title: Antidepressant follow-up documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports documenting low mood, loss of interest, duration, course, functional, interpersonal and social difficulty, previous mood history, physical and mental comorbidity, sleep, stressful events, medicines, substances, loneliness and support.
assessment.rationale: Supports symptoms, function, context and comprehensive assessment for antidepressant follow-up; no decision is generated.

source.title: Depression in adults: treatment and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE mental-health, crisis, referral, and legal adaptation
source.population: Adults with possible or diagnosed depression; communication and carer involvement are qualified.
source.setting: Primary and mental healthcare recognition and assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting mood and interest screening, duration, course, function, social and interpersonal context, sleep, substances, medicines, loneliness and clinician-completed risk assessment. It does not diagnose depression or generate a referral or treatment.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.7 — recognition, comprehensive assessment, function, and context

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting low mood, loss of interest, duration, course, functional, interpersonal and social difficulty, previous mood history, physical and mental comorbidity, sleep, stressful events, medicines, substances, loneliness and support.

assessment.text: Supports recording that suicidal ideation, intent, support and immediate risk were directly assessed by a competent clinician; it does not populate a negative, assign risk, or generate escalation.
assessment.rationale: Supports clinician-completed self-harm and suicide risk assessment for antidepressant follow-up; no decision is generated.

source.title: Depression in adults: treatment and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE mental-health, crisis, referral, and legal adaptation
source.population: Adults with possible or diagnosed depression; communication and carer involvement are qualified.
source.setting: Primary and mental healthcare recognition and assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting mood and interest screening, duration, course, function, social and interpersonal context, sleep, substances, medicines, loneliness and clinician-completed risk assessment. It does not diagnose depression or generate a referral or treatment.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports recording that suicidal ideation, intent, support and immediate risk were directly assessed by a competent clinician; it does not populate a negative, assign risk, or generate escalation.

assessment.text: Supports structured review of all medicines, views, understanding, concerns, questions, adherence context, adverse-reaction or side-effect context, monitoring, and circumstances for seeking professional advice.
assessment.rationale: Supports medicine use, adherence, benefit, adverse effects and monitoring for antidepressant follow-up; no decision is generated.

source.title: Medicines optimisation: the safe and effective use of medicines to enable the best possible outcomes — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE medicines, prescribing, pharmacy, discharge, and information-governance adaptation
source.population: Adults, children, and young people using medicines, with specific transfer, reconciliation, and decision-involvement recommendations.
source.setting: Transfers between care settings and clinician-led medicines consultations.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting current medicines, changes made during transfer, reconciliation, discrepancies, person or carer involvement, values, preferences, and clinician-led decisions. It does not generate a medicine start, stop, dose, prescription, or deprescribing decision.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports structured review of all medicines, views, understanding, concerns, questions, adherence context, adverse-reaction or side-effect context, monitoring, and circumstances for seeking professional advice.
